definition: Public tree layer contains branch labels and geographic metadata sufficient for source-adequacy testing and receiver–source Q2 evaluation
use_in_analysis: Can support source-receiver inference under predefined geographic masks

definition: Public tree layer supports source-adequacy assessment and direct-child country structure, but not terminal branch–country Q2 evaluation
use_in_analysis: Can support source-adequacy assessment and triangulation, but not full terminal-level Q2 evaluation

definition: Dataset has dense receiver-region geographic coverage but insufficient source-side observations
use_in_analysis: Supports receiver-region structure but not full source-receiver replication

definition: Tree topology is visible, but geography-bearing terminal observations are unavailable or insufficient

A new lineage-level result can be treated as load-bearing evidence only if source and receiver terminal counts are each at least 25, source and receiver country counts are each at least 3, the source-adequacy statistic is greater than 1, the predefined diversity-contrast statistic D is negative, the upper bound of the equal-n rarefaction interval remains below 0, the predefined geographic permutation test yields p ≤ 0.05, and the preassigned lineage role is neither support-only nor boundary
use_in_analysis: Rows that do not meet all conditions remain classified as supporting, boundary, or no-call rather than being promoted on the basis of directional compatibility alone

definition: The comparison is directionally compatible at broad public labels but does not meet branch-unit reliability criteria after compression
use_in_analysis: Reported as support-only evidence rather than validation

definition: The comparison is historically relevant but does not satisfy the criteria for load-bearing source-receiver inference
use_in_analysis: Reported as a scope boundary rather than as validation or model failure

definition: Source-side terminal or branch representation is insufficient for the stated test

term: No-call: overlapping source and receiver masks
definition: Source and receiver masks cannot be separated without post hoc or arbitrary tuning
The lineage root is too broad for the stated source-receiver model
Published ancient-DNA sampling does not satisfy the predefined geographic, chronological, phylogenetic-elevation, and directness requirements
use_in_analysis: Absence from currently published ancient-DNA sampling is not treated as direct negative evidence
